rulings.Case Title: CHARLES LI VS. MEI LING FANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Demas Yan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena Duces Tecum As To Demas Yan, Meiling Fang, Oakland Development Llc, Legal Associates Inc., Legal Recovery Llc, Legal Recover Llc, And T24 Llc
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 02, 2021 line 1. Defendant Demas Yan'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Quashing Subpoena Duces Tecum As To Demas Yan, Meiling Fang, Oakland Development Llc, Legal Associates Inc., Legal Recovery Llc, Legal Recover Llc, And T24 LLC is DENIED. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted. Defendant shall remit to Plaintiff's counsel $4,600 within 10 days of notice of entry of order. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MOBILEONE LLC VS. FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 02, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT ANGELA OLIVERI, FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts taking this matter outside the statute of frauds; allege facts supporting a cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty as applicable under the circumstances of this case; and to allege misrepresentation by these defendants with requisite particularity. All amendments to be in bold font. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: MOBILEONE LLC VS. FIRST WESTERN PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 02, 2021 line 2. DEFENDANT SCOTT LAEBER, COLLIERS INTERNATIONAL CA, INC. DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is sustained. Demurrer to the causes of action for intentional and negligent misrepresentation is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, establishing each element of these cause of action, including "who, what, when, where, and how." Demurrer to the cause of action for concealment is sustained with final leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions, showing that this defendant knew about zoning issues. Demurrer to the cause of action for breach of contract is sustained with final leave to amend to allege a contract between Plaintiff and these defendants, the contractual clause breached and damages. Letter of Intent is not a contract between Plaintiff and this defendant. To the extent causes of action for professional negligence and breach of fiduciary duty are based on the failure to investigate, demurrer to these causes of action is sustained without leave to amend. If plaintiff is able re-allege these causes of action based on facts/theory other than failure to investigate, Plaintiff shall have final leave to amend to so allege. All amendments to be in bold font. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: JAMES ELLIS JOHNSON VS. SAN FRANCISCO RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In The Demurrer Filed By Respondent San Francisco Residential Rent Stablization And Arbitration Board
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Jun 02, 2021 line 3. REAL PARTY IN INTEREST GREEN TREE PROPERTY MANAGEMENT Notice Of Joinder In The Demurrer Filed By Respondent San Francisco Residential Rent Stablization And Arbitration Board is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the Proof of Service for the agency's final decision and its legal effect. Petition was filed (and it appears based on the arguments in opposition that it was attempted to be filed) more than 90 days after service. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
